criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the MASK knowledge formulation as paired multimodal representations {(w_k, v_k)} for K semantic concepts.
The plan includes constructing the Prototype-Aware Encoder (PAE) h to extract region mean μ and variance σ from raw region features.
The plan includes computing prototypical region representations v_k by averaging all related region representations of the same semantic concept.
The code for the PAE model h is implemented as one FC layer followed by three self-attention layers outputting both mean μ and variance σ as specified in Equation 3 and Table 8.
The code for computing prototypical region representations v_k by averaging all region representations {μ_j} belonging to the k-th concept is implemented as Equation 2.

The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the Image Embedding Branch including the PAE encoder, the FRM module, the information retention loss, and the prototype consistency contrastive learning loss.
The plan includes the Feature Restoration Module (FRM) g to reconstruct raw region representations from the latent space (μ, σ, z).
The code for the FRM model g is implemented with a self-attention layer and two FC layers reconstructing R' from μ, σ, and a random vector z as Equation 4.
The plan includes the information retention loss L_ir combining KL divergence D_KL(N(μ,σ²)||N(0,1)) and an L2 reconstruction term.

The code for the information retention loss L_ir is implemented with KL divergence between the latent distribution and the standard normal, plus the L2 reconstruction error between R and R', as Equation 5.
The plan includes the prototype consistency contrastive learning loss L_cl using prototypes as class centers with a temperature hyperparameter τ.

The code for the prototype consistency contrastive learning loss L_cl is implemented as Equation 6, computing log-softmax over dot products of prototypes v_k and region representations μ_n with temperature τ.
The agent has read Section 3.3 completely to understand the Text Embedding Branch and the Modality Transfer Model (MTM) f.
The plan includes the Modality Transfer Model (MTM) f with three self-attention layers and three FC layers to map region representations into the word embedding space.
The code for the MTM model f is implemented with FC + ReLU + 3 Self-Attention + FC + ReLU + Self-Attention + FC layers mapping μ to predicted word embeddings V' (300-dim) as Equation 7 and Table 8.

The plan includes the cross-modal alignment loss L_cm with a word alignment term and a structure-preserving term that enforces cosine similarity relations in μ-space to be reflected in the word embedding space.

The code for the cross-modal alignment loss L_cm is implemented as Equation 9 with (1) a word alignment term: expectation of (1 - cos(w_i/||w_i||, w_i'/||w_i'||)), and (2) a structure-preserving term: expectation of squared difference between cosine similarities of predicted embeddings and region representations.
The agent has read Section 3.4 completely to understand the knowledge-based image-text matching procedure including the global similarity score and OOD word prototype construction.
The plan includes computing the global similarity score using max-mean pooling ρ(μ·U^T) over region-to-prototype dot products.
The code for the global similarity score s = ρ(μ·U^T) is implemented with max-mean pooling (max along columns then mean along rows) as Equation 10.
The plan includes constructing prototypical region representations for OOD words by computing softmax similarity scores of the OOD word embedding against m sampled known word embeddings.
The code for computing OOD word similarity scores {s_q} via softmax over dot products of w_out with sampled word embeddings {w_q} is implemented as Equation 11.
The code for constructing the OOD prototypical region representation v_out as a weighted sum of sampled prototypes v_q with similarity weights s_q is implemented as Equation 12.

The code for selecting the top-m nearest neighbors to an OOD word by computing cosine similarities between normalized OOD embedding and all normalized knowledge embeddings is implemented as Equation 13.
The agent has read Section 3.5 completely to understand the training data collection from Visual Genome and the total training loss formulation.
The plan includes using the Visual Genome (VG) dataset to collect nouns/adjectives and associated image regions as the paired training data for MASK.
The code for the total training loss L = L_ir + λ1·L_cm + λ2·L_cl with trade-off factors λ1 and λ2 is implemented as Equation 14.
The agent has read Section 3.6 completely to understand the re-ranking extension combining Z-Score normalized similarity vectors from an existing model and MASK using balancing factor α.
The plan includes the re-ranking method computing combined similarity s_hat^k = ZS(s_tilde^k) + α·ZS(s^k) with Z-Score normalization for the top-k retrieved candidates.
The code for the re-ranking formula s_hat^k = ZS(s_tilde^k) + α·ZS(s^k) is implemented with Z-Score normalization of both similarity vectors as Equation 15.

The agent has read Appendix I.2 completely to check the implementation details: K=12,385 concepts, I=36 regions per image, M=2048 feature dimension, glove-840B-300d word vectors, batch sizes 4096/2048, λ1=λ2=3, m=10, Adam optimizer with lr=1e-4.
The plan includes collecting K=12,385 semantic concepts from the VG dataset by filtering out special characters and rare words.
The code for loading and filtering the VG dataset to obtain K=12,385 semantic concepts with associated word synsets and image regions has been implemented.
The plan includes using the pre-trained Faster-RCNN (BUTD) with I=36 detected regions per image and M=2048 dimensional raw region features.
The code for extracting region features using the pre-trained Faster-RCNN (BUTD) model with 36 regions per image and 2048-dimensional features has been implemented using Detectron2.
The plan includes using the pretrained GloVe word vectors (glove-840B-300d) to obtain 300-dimensional word embeddings.
The code for loading the pretrained GloVe word vectors (glove-840B-300d) to produce 300-dimensional word embeddings w_k has been implemented.
The plan includes the training schedule with batch size 4096 for the first 200 epochs and batch size 2048 for the following 200 epochs.
The code for training with Adam optimizer (lr=1e-4), batch size 4096 for the first 200 epochs and 2048 for the next 200 epochs, with λ1=λ2=3 and sampling size m=10 has been implemented.
The plan includes using NLTK tokenization to parse nouns and adjectives from text descriptions for knowledge-based matching.
The code for NLTK-based tokenization to extract words from text captions as input to the knowledge-based matching module has been implemented.
The agent has read Appendix I.4 completely to understand the exact PAE, FRM, and MTM architectures with input/output dimensions and parameter counts from Table 8.
The PAE model architecture is implemented with FC (2048→512) + ReLU + 3 Self-Attention layers (512→512) + Layer Normalization, outputting both mean μ and variance σ in 512-dimensional latent space.
The FRM model architecture is implemented with FC (512→512) + ReLU + Self-Attention (512→512) + FC (512→2048) to reconstruct 2048-dimensional raw region representations.

The MTM model architecture is implemented with FC (512→300) + ReLU + Self-Attention (300→300) + Self-Attention (300→300) + FC (300→300) + ReLU + Self-Attention (300→300) + FC (300→300) outputting 300-dimensional predicted word embeddings.
The agent has read Section 4.1 and Appendix I.1 completely to understand the Flickr30K (31,783 images) and MSCOCO (123,287 images) dataset splits and evaluation metrics.
The plan includes loading Flickr30K with official splits of 29,783 training / 1,000 validation / 1,000 testing images, each with 5 captions.
The plan includes loading MSCOCO with official splits of 113,287 training / 5,000 validation / 5,000 testing images, each with 5 captions.
The code for loading the Flickr30K and MSCOCO datasets with their respective official train/val/test splits has been implemented.
The plan includes evaluation using R@1, R@5, R@10 for both image retrieval and image annotation tasks, plus Rs (sum of all six recall values).
The code for computing R@1, R@5, R@10 for both image retrieval and image annotation, and the aggregate score Rs (sum of all six recall rates) has been implemented.

The agent has read Table 1 completely to check the unpaired image-text matching results comparing model-based methods (CHAN, DSRLN, CORA, BOOM, 3SHNet) and knowledge-based methods (MACK, MACK^VG-M, MASK).
The plan includes implementing knowledge-based baseline methods MACK and MACK^VG-M for comparison in unpaired image-text matching.
The code for the complete unpaired image-text matching pipeline using MASK (PAE + FRM + MTM + knowledge-based matching) on Flickr30K and MSCOCO has been implemented.
The code for the main unpaired image-text matching experiment with MASK on Flickr30K and MSCOCO has been executed successfully without errors.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: MASK achieves Rs=122.8 on Flickr30K (Image Retrieval R@1=4.8, R@5=14.8, R@10=22.0; Image Annotation R@1=12.1, R@5=30.1, R@10=39.0).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: MASK achieves Rs=209.5 on MSCOCO (Image Retrieval R@1=7.6, R@5=26.7, R@10=41.8; Image Annotation R@1=22.7, R@5=48.5, R@10=62.2).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: MASK outperforms knowledge-based baselines MACK (Rs=95.3 Flickr30K, Rs=201.7 MSCOCO) and MACK^VG-M (Rs=104.8 Flickr30K, Rs=205.2 MSCOCO).
The agent has read Table 2 completely to check the zero-shot image-text matching re-ranking results using CLIP and ALBEF as base models.
The plan includes re-ranking CLIP and ALBEF similarity outputs using MASK with the balancing factor α on both Flickr30K and MSCOCO.
The code for zero-shot image-text matching by re-ranking CLIP and ALBEF outputs with MASK using Z-Score normalization and α has been implemented.
The code for zero-shot re-ranking experiments with CLIP and ALBEF using MASK has been executed successfully without errors on both datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: CLIP+MASK achieves Rs=534.3 on Flickr30K and Rs=400.4 on MSCOCO, outperforming CLIP+MACK^VG-M (Rs=531.6 and Rs=394.4).
The execution outcomes match Table 2: ALBEF+MASK achieves Rs=518.1 on Flickr30K and Rs=436.2 on MSCOCO, outperforming ALBEF+MACK^VG-M (Rs=513.4 and Rs=432.2).
The agent has read Table 3 completely to understand the ablation study on OOD words and the three loss components (L_ir, L_cm, L_cl).
The plan includes ablation experiments: MASK without OOD words, MASK without L_cm, and MASK without L_cl.
The code for the three ablation variants (MASK w/o OOD words, MASK w/o L_cm, MASK w/o L_cl) has been implemented by appropriately disabling each component.
The code for the OOD words ablation and loss component ablation experiments has been executed successfully without errors on both Flickr30K and MSCOCO.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: removing L_cl causes the largest performance drop (Rs 122.8→92.4 on Flickr30K and 209.5→123.5 on MSCOCO), confirming L_cl's critical contribution.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: removing OOD words reduces Rs from 122.8 to 116.2 on Flickr30K and from 209.5 to 193.1 on MSCOCO.
The agent has read Table 4 completely to understand the hyperparameter ratio analysis with λ1/λ2 = 0.3, 1.0, and 3.0.
The plan includes hyperparameter ratio experiments with λ1/λ2 ∈ {0.3, 1.0, 3.0} on both Flickr30K and MSCOCO.
The code for hyperparameter ratio experiments with λ1/λ2 ∈ {0.3, 1.0, 3.0} has been implemented.
The code for hyperparameter ratio experiments has been executed successfully without errors on both datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: λ1/λ2=1.0 achieves the best performance (Rs=122.8 on Flickr30K and Rs=209.5 on MSCOCO), showing that balanced weighting of L_cm and L_cl is optimal.
The agent has read Table 5 completely to understand the sampling size analysis with m ∈ {1, 10, 50, 100}.
The plan includes sampling size experiments with m ∈ {1, 10, 50, 100} to study the effect on OOD prototype quality.
The code for sampling size experiments with m ∈ {1, 10, 50, 100} has been implemented.
The code for sampling size experiments has been executed successfully without errors on both Flickr30K and MSCOCO.

The execution outcomes match Table 5: performance follows a rise-then-fall trend with best at m=50 (Rs=128.1 on Flickr30K and Rs=222.6 on MSCOCO), while m=10 and m=100 yield Rs=122.8 and Rs=125.4 on Flickr30K.
The agent has read Table 6 completely to understand the re-ranking balancing factor α analysis with α ∈ {0, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2}.
The plan includes re-ranking α experiments with α ∈ {0, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2} for MASK (CLIP) on both datasets.
The code for re-ranking experiments with different α values (0, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2) has been implemented.
The code for α analysis experiments has been executed successfully without errors on both datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 6: α=0.15 achieves best performance (Rs=534.3 on Flickr30K and Rs=400.4 on MSCOCO) for CLIP+MASK, confirming that a small α value is optimal.
The agent has read Appendix J.1 and Table 9 completely to understand the cross-dataset image-text matching setup using VSRN and SAEM.
The plan includes cross-dataset re-ranking experiments: VSRN and SAEM trained on one dataset (MSCOCO or Flickr30K) and tested on the other, re-ranked by MASK.
The code for cross-dataset re-ranking experiments using VSRN and SAEM models with MASK has been implemented.
The code for cross-dataset experiments has been executed successfully without errors for both MSCOCO→Flickr30K and Flickr30K→MSCOCO directions.
The execution outcomes match Table 9: VSRN+MASK achieves Rs=436.3 on MSCOCO→Flickr30K and Rs=232.4 on Flickr30K→MSCOCO, outperforming VSRN+FR (Rs=429.1 and Rs=223.7).
The agent has read Appendix J.3 and Table 11 completely to understand the different detector experiments (DETR, DINO, BUTD, BUTD+).
The plan includes detector comparison experiments with DETR, DINO (with and without VG pretraining), BUTD, and BUTD+ (ResNet152, ConvNeXt, Swin backbone) within the MASK framework.
The code for integrating different detectors (DETR, DINO, BUTD, BUTD+) into the MASK framework for feature extraction has been implemented.
The code for different detector comparison experiments has been executed successfully without errors on Flickr30K.

The execution outcomes match Table 11: BUTD achieves Rs=122.8, BUTD+(ResNet152, 36 boxes) achieves Rs=136.1, and BUTD+(Swin, 36 boxes) achieves Rs=152.3, while DINO without VG pretraining achieves only Rs=21.6.
The agent has read Appendix J.4 and Table 12 completely to understand the model architecture analysis with varying numbers of FC and self-attention layers.
The plan includes architecture analysis experiments with different configurations of FC layers and self-attention layers for the PAE, FRM, and MTM models.
The code for model architecture experiments with varying layer configurations for PAE, FRM, and MTM has been implemented to allow flexible architecture specification.
The code for model architecture experiments has been executed successfully without errors on the Flickr30K dataset.

The execution outcomes match Table 12: the default PAE (1 FC + 3 Self-Attention, Rs=122.8) outperforms the 3-FC variant (3 FC + 3 Self-Attention, Rs=103.4), showing FC layers degrade performance when overused.
The agent has read Appendix J.5 and Table 13 completely to understand the different pretrained word vector comparison (GloVe, Word2Vec, FastText).
The plan includes word vector comparison experiments with GloVe (glove-840B-300d), Word2Vec, and FastText on both Flickr30K and MSCOCO.
The code for word vector comparison experiments using GloVe, Word2Vec, and FastText as alternatives to produce 300-dimensional word embeddings has been implemented.
The code for different word vector experiments has been executed successfully without errors on both datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 13: GloVe (Rs=122.8 Flickr30K, Rs=209.5 MSCOCO) outperforms Word2Vec (Rs=117.6, Rs=206.8) and FastText (Rs=108.4, Rs=201.1).
The agent has read Appendix H completely to understand the optional domain knowledge fine-tuning via bidirectional region-word cycle-consistent learning on unpaired data.
The plan includes the optional fine-tuning step using bidirectional cycle-consistent learning (R2W and W2R) to learn a transformation matrix W for domain adaptation.

The code for the bidirectional cycle-consistent fine-tuning is implemented: similarity matrix S = uW(μW)^T, reconstructed word representations u_hat = softmax(S)μW, and reconstructed region representations μ_hat = softmax(S^T)uW as Equations 88-89.
The code for the self-supervised fine-tuning loss L_ss = L^R2W + L^W2R using cross-entropy between predicted and ground-truth labels in both R2W and W2R directions is implemented as Equations 90-93.
The code for the extended hyperparameter experiments varying λ1 and λ2 independently (values 1.0, 3.0, 5.0 each) has been executed on both datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 15: the best performance is achieved at λ1=λ2=3.0 (Rs=122.8 on Flickr30K and Rs=209.5 on MSCOCO), with performance degrading when either λ1 or λ2 is set to 5.0.
